99: **Norway**, 2nd in **Group I** on 6 points, arrive at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami having beaten **Brazil** 2-1 in the last 16 courtesy of an Erling Haaland brace — his seventh and eighth goals of the tournament — while **England**, top of **Group L** on 7 points, edged co-hosts **Mexico** 3-2 at the Azteca with Jude Bellingham scoring twice and Harry Kane converting a penalty despite playing the final half-hour with ten men. This is the first meeting between the nations at a major tournament finals in 12 previous all-time encounters — England lead that record seven wins to two — and **England** face it without suspended Jarell Quansah (red card vs Mexico), the injured Jordan Henderson (wrist surgery), a doubtful Marc Guehi (hamstring), and Reece James, who has missed three games with a hamstring strain but is expected to return. **Norway** doctor Ola Sand confirmed the full squad is fit after a sickness scare swept the camp post-Brazil, and a **Norway** win would send them to a semi-final against the winner of **Argentina** vs **Switzerland**.
100: **Argentina**, top of **Group J** on 9 points and unbeaten in 11 consecutive tournament matches — their longest such streak in history — meet **Switzerland**, top of **Group B** on 7 points, in Kansas City in a quarter-final that is only their third-ever meeting at this stage; **Argentina** won both previous clashes, 2-0 in 1966 and 1-0 in 2014. Lionel Messi, who became the first player to score in six consecutive knockout matches and now holds 14 knockout-stage goal contributions — a 60-year record — after his comeback goal against Egypt, has never lost to **Switzerland** across all competitions, and **Switzerland** have never beaten **Argentina** in seven all-time attempts. Three **Switzerland** players are ruled out — Johan Manzambi (knee), Michael Aebischer and Luca Jaquez — while **Argentina** report no major injury concerns, though Messi has now missed two penalties in the same tournament, an unprecedented record. The winner faces **Norway** or **England** in the semi-finals.
98: **Spain**, top of **Group H** on 7 points and the only side yet to concede at these finals, face **Belgium** — level with one other team on 5 points and position not yet separated — at Los Angeles Stadium, with this only the third competitive meeting between the nations and the second at this tournament stage, **Belgium** winning in 1986 and **Spain** triumphing in the 1990 edition. Amadou Onana is ruled out for **Belgium** after rupturing his ACL against the United States, Zeno Debast has not featured due to a leg injury, and Nico Williams (adductor) plus Yeremy Pino (collarbone) are both serious doubts for **Spain** after being injured in the same group match against Uruguay. Mikel Oyarzabal leads **Spain**'s attack with four tournament goals, Mikel Merino's 91st-minute finish eliminated Portugal in the last 16 to set up **Spain**'s sixth quarter-final appearance, and Kevin De Bruyne — who had been substituted in each of his four previous appearances — is expected to start for **Belgium** having been dropped against the US. The winner advances to a semi-final against **France**.
